name: Lustre Striping Layout Planning
summary: Inspect and plan Lustre stripe layouts for data-intensive HPC workloads.
description: This skill helps users and support teams collect read-only Lustre layout evidence with lfs commands and prepare reviewable stripe-count, stripe-size, pool, and progressive-file-layout plans before changing directories, creating large files, or migrating existing data.

tools.name: lfs
tools.notes: Used for read-only getstripe and filesystem layout inspection; setstripe commands are generated for review only.

tools.name: find
tools.notes: Optionally samples large files in a target directory with bounded depth.

inputs.name: target_path
inputs.description: Existing Lustre file or directory whose current layout and filesystem state should be inspected.

inputs.name: output_dir
inputs.description: Local directory where read-only evidence and the reviewable layout plan should be written.

inputs.name: expected_io_pattern
inputs.description: Expected workload pattern, such as many small files, one large shared checkpoint, per-rank outputs, or staged analysis reads.

inputs.name: site_policy
inputs.description: Local policy confirming whether users may set Lustre stripe layouts, pools, PFL templates, or migrate existing files.

outputs.name: lustre_layout_report
outputs.description: Read-only report with lfs getstripe, lfs df, df, stat, and bounded directory sample evidence.

outputs.name: stripe_layout_plan
outputs.description: Reviewable notes and placeholder lfs setstripe commands that are not executed by the example script.

outputs.name: site_policy_checklist
outputs.description: Checklist for storage policy, existing data, pools, PFL support, and rollback expectations.

examples.title: Review local Lustre striping policy before changes

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check shell syntax without requiring a Lustre filesystem.
Run the report script against a public-safe Lustre path and confirm it writes evidence and a review-only stripe plan without modifying the target path.
